commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Up. The same term only occurs again, (Lamentations iii. 8,) where it may have the same sense, though the Septuagint, give it here a quite opposite meaning, "the man whose eyes are open "the prophet. But Balaam alludes to his not being able to see the angel as soon as his ass, as he does, ver. 4., and chap. xxii. 31. (Calmet)
